title: How to Roll — The Core Mechanic
body:
Roll *d100* (two ten-sided dice read as a percentage, 00–99) under your relevant Stat or Save to succeed.

• *Advantage [+]:* Roll twice, take the lower result.
• *Disadvantage [-]:* Roll twice, take the higher result.
• *90–99 always fails*, even with Advantage.
• *Critical Success:* matching doubles that fall under your stat (e.g. 33 when stat is 45).
• *Critical Failure:* matching doubles that fall over your stat (e.g. 77 when stat is 45).

title: The Four Stats
body:
Roll *2d10+25* for each of the four stats at character creation. Average result: 36.

• *Strength* — Physical feats, melee damage base (Str ÷ 10, round down).
• *Speed* — Initiative order, evasion, chases.
• *Intellect* — Knowledge, problem-solving, technology.
• *Combat* — Accuracy when attacking with weapons.

title: The Three Saves
body:
Roll *2d10+10* for each save at character creation.

• *Sanity* — Resists psychological horror, alien incomprehensibility, madness.
• *Fear* — Resists immediate dread, panic triggers, terrifying revelations. Also used for Panic Checks.
• *Body* — Resists physical trauma, toxins, decompression, disease.

title: Character Creation — 9 Steps
body:
1. *Roll Stats* — 2d10+25 each for Strength, Speed, Intellect, Combat.
2. *Roll Saves* — 2d10+10 each for Sanity, Fear, Body.
3. *Choose Class* — Apply class stat/save bonuses.
4. *Roll Health* — 1d10+10 = Maximum Health. Start at Max with 0 Wounds.
5. *Gain Stress* — Current Stress and Minimum Stress both start at 2.
6. *Note Trauma Response* — Mark your class Trauma Response.
7. *Choose Skills* — Each class has preloaded skills plus bonus picks.
8. *Roll Loadout, Trinket & Patch* — d10 on class table; d100 for Trinket and Patch.
9. *Finishing* — Write name and pronouns. Mark 0 above High Score. Ready to play.

title: Starting Credits
body:
Roll *2d10×10cr* in addition to your Starting Loadout.

If you forgo your Loadout entirely, roll *2d10×100cr* instead.

Credits are tight — you are working-class spacers, not heroes.

title: Initiative & Turn Order
body:
*Rounds* — Combat runs in ~10 second intervals. Everything in a round happens at roughly the same time.

*Each Round:*
1. Warden describes the situation and likely outcome if no one acts.
2. Players describe how their characters react.
3. Everyone commits to a course of action.
4. Warden resolves all actions simultaneously, calling for any Stat Checks or Saves.
5. Everyone rolls. Damage and Wounds are rolled after.
6. Warden describes the new situation. Next round begins.

*Strict Turn Order (optional):* At the start of an encounter, everyone makes a *Speed Check*. Those who succeed act before enemy hostiles; those who fail act after. If the situation changes dramatically, Speed Checks can be called again.

*Surprise:* If characters may be ambushed or shocked, the Warden calls for a *Fear Save*. Those who succeed can react. Those who fail are too shocked to act until the next round.

title: Making an Attack
body:
On your turn, make a *Combat Check*: roll d100 under your Combat stat.

• *Success* → roll weapon damage.
• *Failure* → the attack misses.
• *Critical Success* (matching doubles under) → something exceptional happens.
• *Critical Failure* (matching doubles over) → something bad happens (weapon jams, friendly fire, etc.).
• *(AA) Auto-Attack weapons* hit automatically — no Combat Check needed, just roll damage.

title: Damage & Armor Points
body:
After a successful hit:

1. Roll the weapon's damage dice.
2. Subtract the target's *AP (Armor Points)*.
3. Remaining damage is dealt to *Health*.

AP regenerates between encounters. Advanced Battle Dress also applies *Damage Reduction 3* after AP (subtract another 3 from the remaining damage).

title: Taking a Wound
body:
When Health drops to *0*:

1. Take 1 Wound.
2. Roll on the appropriate *Wound Table* (determined by the weapon that dealt the killing blow: Bleeding, Gore, Gunshot, Blunt Force, or Fire/Explosives).
3. Reset Health to Maximum minus any carryover damage.

When Wounds exceed your *Max Wounds* (base 2; Marines and Androids: 3), you *die*. Permadeath — no resurrection.

title: Wound Types
body:
Each weapon deals a specific wound type on the Wound Table:

• *Bleeding* — Scalpel, Hand Welder, Rigging Gun, Laser Cutter, Vibechete
• *Gore* — Boarding Axe, Laser Cutter [+], Vibechete
• *Gunshot* — Combat Shotgun, Revolver, Pulse Rifle, SMG, Smart Rifle, GPMG
• *Blunt Force* — Crowbar, Foam Gun, Stun Baton, Tranq Pistol, Unarmed
• *Fire/Explosives* — Flamethrower, Flare Gun, Frag Grenade

Some weapons specify [+] or [-] on the wound roll. Vibechete causes a Wound on *both* Bleeding AND Gore columns simultaneously.

title: Stress — How It Works
body:
Stress is the core tension engine of Mothership.

• *Current Stress* starts at 2.
• *Minimum Stress* starts at 2 — it cannot go lower.
• Stress *increases* from horror, failed saves, and bad events.
• Whenever Current Stress *equals or exceeds* Minimum Stress, make a *Panic Check* (Fear Save).
• On a failed Panic Check, roll on the *Panic Table*.
• Stress *decreases* during Shore Leave between missions.

title: Panic Check — Step by Step
body:
1. Your Current Stress equals or exceeds your Minimum Stress.
2. Make a *Fear Save* (roll d100 under your Fear stat).
3. *Success* → No panic. Breathe.
4. *Failure* → Roll on the *Panic Table* and apply the result immediately.
5. Your class *Trauma Response* may force nearby crew to make their own Fear Saves.

*Teamster bonus:* Once per session, may take Advantage [+] on a Panic Check.

title: Trauma Responses by Class
body:
Each class has a Trauma Response that cascades panic to nearby crew:

• *Marine* — When you panic, every nearby friendly crew must make a Fear Save.
• *Android* — Fear Saves made by nearby crew are at Disadvantage [-].
• *Scientist* — When you fail a Sanity Save, all nearby crew gain 1 Stress.
• *Teamster* — Once per session, may take Advantage [+] on one Panic Check (beneficial, not harmful).

title: Oxygen & Survival
body:
Track O2 carefully in hostile environments:

• *Vaccsuit* — 12 hours O2. Short-range comms, headlamp, radiation shielding. If punctured, decompression within 1d5 rounds.
• *Hazard Suit* — 1 hour O2. Air filter, extreme temperature protection.
• *Advanced Battle Dress* — 1 hour O2.

*Decompression in vacuum:* Body Save or take 1 Wound (Fire/Explosives) per round.

title: Skill Tiers
body:
Skills have three tiers. You must take the prerequisite tier first:

• *Trained (+10)* — Basic competency. Adds +10 to the relevant stat check.
• *Expert (+15)* — Advanced competency. Adds +15. Requires Trained in the tree.
• *Master (+20)* — Specialist. Adds +20. Requires Expert in the tree.

Skill bonuses stack with stat values. You can only add one Skill Bonus to a Ship Stat Check at a time.

title: Skill Trees
body:
Three tiers: *Trained* (+10), *Expert* (+15), *Master* (+20). Any listed prerequisite is sufficient.

*Trained → Expert*
Linguistics or Zoology → Psychology
Zoology or Botany → Pathology
Zoology → Field Medicine
Botany or Geology → Ecology
Botany → Wilderness Survival
Geology or Industrial Equipment → Asteroid Mining
Industrial Equipment or Jury-Rigging → Mechanical Repair
Jury-Rigging or Chemistry or Military Training → Explosives
Chemistry → Pharmacology
Computers → Hacking
Zero-G → Piloting
Mathematics → Physics
Art or Archaeology or Theology → Mysticism
Military Training or Rimwise → Firearms
Military Training or Rimwise or Athletics → Hand-to-Hand Combat

*Expert → Master*
Psychology → Sophontology
Pathology → Exobiology
Pathology or Field Medicine → Surgery
Ecology or Asteroid Mining → Planetology
Mechanical Repair → Robotics, Engineering, Cybernetics
Hacking → Artificial Intelligence
Piloting or Physics or Mysticism → Hyperspace
Piloting or Firearms → Command
Mysticism → Xenoesotericism

title: Class Starting Skills
body:
Each class starts with preloaded skills and additional picks:

• *Marine* — Preloaded: Military Training, Athletics. Bonus: 1 Expert Skill OR 2 Trained Skills.
• *Android* — Preloaded: Linguistics, Computers, Mathematics. Bonus: 1 Expert Skill OR 2 Trained Skills.
• *Scientist* — Preloaded: 1 Master Skill + 1 Expert + 1 Trained. Bonus: 1 Trained Skill.
• *Teamster* — Preloaded: Industrial Equipment, Zero-G. Bonus: 1 Trained + 1 Expert Skill.

title: Ship Stat Checks
body:
Ships have three stats that function as both stats and saves:

• *Thrusters* — Maneuvering and acceleration. Like Speed.
• *Weapons* — Targeting and attacking other ships. Like Combat.
• *Systems* — Sensors, computers, maintenance, other subsystems.

Whenever a ship *fails* a Stat Check, *everyone aboard gains 1 Stress*.
90–99 always fails. Critical Failure = everyone aboard makes a Panic Check.

One crew member may add their relevant Skill Bonus to a Ship Stat Check at a time.

title: Ship Combat
body:
Ship combat follows the same structure as personal combat:

1. *Initiative* — Highest Thrusters acts first.
2. *Attack* — Roll d100 under Weapons stat.
3. *Damage* — Ship weapons deal Megadamage (MDMG) dice.
4. *Critical Hits* — Roll on the Status Report (Megadamage Track).
5. *Failed Ship Saves* — Everyone aboard gains 1 Stress.
6. *Critical Failure* — Everyone makes a Panic Check.
7. *Escape* — Thrusters Check to disengage.

title: Transponder
body:
Every ship broadcasts an automated Transponder signal containing: Ship Identifier/name, make/model, jump drive info, ship class and type.

*Illegal (or highly suspect) to turn off* in most star systems. Disabling a transponder is a red flag to authorities and other ships.

title: The TOMBS Cycle
body:
Every Horror in Mothership follows this 5-act structure:

• *Act I — Transgression:* Someone awakens the Horror. A line was crossed.
• *Act II — Omens:* Signs of the Horror's approach. Build tension.
• *Act III — Manifestation:* The Horror reveals its true form.
• *Act IV — Banishment:* Players race to fight back. Stakes escalate.
• *Act V — Slumber:* The Horror relents — for now. The cycle begins again.

Every Horror, in every module, follows this structure. Design your scenario around it.

title: Session Prep — 10 Steps
body:
1. Get organized — label notebook: Index, Roster, Session Prep.
2. Invite 3–4 friends. Schedule 3–4 hours.
3. Read the Player's Survival Guide.
4. Choose a Starting Scenario.
5. Note the Setting (roll d10 or choose).
6. Find the Horror — use the Random Horrors Table.
7. Create a Survival Obstacle.
8. Something to Solve / Someone to Save.
9. Map it out with shorthand notes.
10. Find hidden connections, discard weak ideas.

title: The Three Pillars — Survive, Solve, Save
body:
Players can usually only accomplish one, maybe two:

• *Survive the Nightmare* — Outlast the Horror, escape with your lives.
• *Solve the Mystery* — Understand what is happening and why.
• *Save the Day* — Rescue someone, stop the Horror, protect others.

Design scenarios with hard problems and escalating threats. A good story only happens in retrospect — design *situations*, not plots.

title: Obstacle Types
body:
Five categories of obstacles for scenarios:

• *Psychological Trauma* — Creepy environments, isolation, evidence of violence, Omens.
• *Violent Encounters* — Combat. Use sparingly and brutally.
• *Environmental Hazards* — Toxic atmosphere, radiation, zero-G, lack of O2.
• *Social Pressures* — Negotiation, factions, allies, enemies.
• *Resource Scarcity* — O2, food, ammo, fuel, Warp Cores, credits.

title: ABH — Situation Overview
body:
*Planet:* Samsa VI — colonized world with active terraforming.

*The Signal:* Hinton (synthetic science officer) discovered the carcinid Shriek frequency and engineered it into a Signal that expands carcinid reproduction. He broadcasts it from the Orbital Relay, jamming all radio communications on Samsa VI. Anyone attempting radio contact risks Shriek infection.

*Goal:* Discover what's happening, stop the Signal, survive.

*Key locations:* Greta Base (pg.10), Heron Terraforming Station (pg.16), The Carcinid Mothership (pg.30).

*Recommended play:* Scenario 1 → 2 → 3 → 4, but players drive the path.

title: ABH — The Carcinids
body:
*Giant arthropod-insectoid creatures.* Natural weapons and near-impenetrable carapace.

*Carapace:* Bullet and flameproof Aramid Fiber Weave — AP 30. *Weakness:* Hydrofluoric acid.

*The Shriek:* Their primary horror weapon. Triggers a Sanity Save. On failure: +1 Stress and Infection begins (see ABH Rules — Infection Stages).

*Stat block:* Combat 75 / Instinct 75 / Claw 4d10 DMG / Wounds 2 (20 HP) / AP 30.

title: ABH — Infection Stages
body:
Characters exposed to the Shriek who fail a Sanity Save become Infected. Infection progresses in stages:

• *Stage 1:* Recurring headaches, strange compulsions, slight personality shifts.
• *Stage 2:* Hearing the Shriek even in silence, acting in the carcinids' interest without realising.
• *Stage 3:* Full carcinid psychic possession. Character becomes an NPC hostile to the crew.

Radio contact on Samsa VI while the Signal broadcasts: Sanity Save or begin Stage 1.

title: ABH — Stopping the Signal
body:
Two actions required to stop the Signal:

1. *Remove the carc from the controls* in the Control Room [4] at Heron Station — one round action.
2. *Manually reset the Orbital Relay [5]* on the roof — one round action.

Once both are done, radio communications return. Disoriented carcinids retreat: Chimney [11] → Tunnels [15] → The Mothership.

*Roof hazard:* Heavy rain, slippery surface. Falls are lethal.

title: DP — Dead Planet Overview
body:
*Premise:* Somewhere in the universe there is a planet with a dark energy that sucks ships out of hyperspace and strands them in orbit. Jump drives don't work here — only thrusters, which would take hundreds of years to escape.

*The Gaunt:* Nightmare-inducing parasites from a Dead Dimension. Their goal: escape the Dead Planet and flood the universe with necrosis. They do this by compelling captive species to build and open the Dead Gateway.

*Ways to win:* De-power the Dead Gateway, destroy it, blow up the planet, or hitch a ride with Brekt (6 seats).

title: DP — Nightmare Check Rules
body:
A *Sanity Save* made when resting. Frequency depends on location:

• *On the Moon (orbit):* Once a week.
• *On Tyrant Beggar Moon Colony:* Every other night.
• *On the Dead Planet surface:* Every night.

*Failure:* Gain 1 Stress and roll a result from the Nightmare Table.
*Nightmares prevent healing* and the benefits of rest.
*Same nightmare twice:* 1d10 Stress; it becomes your only recurring dream.
*Three failures in a row:* Permanent Night Terrors — gain 1d10[+] Stress every nightmare.

title: DP — Compulsion Mechanic
body:
After two or more failed Nightmare Checks while significantly exposed to the Gaunt:

1. Wake up with a *Compulsion to sculpt* a small terrifying object.
2. Roll on the *Compulsive Nightmarish Sculpture Table* (d10).
3. Have *1d10 days* to complete it. Each day you don't: gain 1d10 Stress.
4. After completing, future Nightmare Checks: take no Stress but must build another, OR take Stress equal to number of sculptures already made.
5. Each sculpture created: Warden rolls d100. If result ≤ total sculptures held by crew → *Gaunt gate opens*.
6. Losing/destroying a sculpture: 1d10 permanent damage to Sanity Save.

title: GD — Gradient Descent Overview
body:
*Location:* THE DEEP — formerly CLOUDBANK Synthetics Production Facility, now controlled by the rogue AI *Monarch*. A megadungeon android factory in rimspace, surrounded by a corporate blockade of Troubleshooters.

*Theme:* Identity horror. Are you who you think you are? Are androids people?

*The Bends mechanic:* Characters may secretly be Infiltrator Androids with implanted human memories. Discuss with all players before using.

*Monarch:* Not simply hostile — alien motivations. It *is* the building. It gains Stress when systems are damaged.

title: GD — The Bends
body:
The Bends measures how certain a character is of their own identity. It operates like Stress but targets identity rather than sanity.

As The Bends increases, a character may:
• Doubt their own memories.
• Act against the group's interests.
• Ultimately *refuse to leave THE DEEP* — becoming an NPC permanently.

*Important:* Talk to all players before introducing Infiltrator Androids or The Bends mechanic. It can be deeply unsettling and is not for every table.

title: APF — Prospero's Dream Overview
body:
*Prospero's Dream* is a massive space station with 8,000,000+ inhabitants. Overcrowded, haphazard, criminal.

*Controlled by:* Golyonova II Bratva (The Novos) — a criminal syndicate run by the secret android *Yandee*.
*Security:* Tempest Mercenary Company.
*Primary economy:* Sycorax drug trade (supplied by Solarian Church, distributed by Teamsters Local 32819L).

*On arrival:* Ships with Hull ≤70 enter dry dock. Hull 71+ moor at piers. All ships need a current manifest. All crew receive an *O2 Credstick* after passing through the Clean Room.

title: APF — Station Laws
body:
*The Laws of The Dream:*

• Have your O2 Credstick at all times. Movements tracked.
• *Contraband (banned):* Laser Cutters, Explosives, Signal Jammers, Contagious Bioweapons, EMP tech.
• Non-hull-piercing weapons permitted. Hull-breaching weapons go in a *rented locker* (1cr/day/weapon).
• Don't enter unauthorized areas, especially The Choke.
• Street justice is pervasive. Major breaches handled by Tempest Co. and The Court.

*Oxygen Tax:* 10cr/day/person. 50cr/day during major events.

title: APF — The Court
body:
Prospero's Dream's legal system: *trial by combat*.

Those who can't pay fees or are arrested appear before Chief Adjudicator *Brunhildh*. Fight in *The Cage* (electrified, revolving stage — Body Save or 1d10 DMG and lose an action).

*Losing* = death or *The Choke* (O2 debtors' prison).
*Winning* = charges dropped.

Power brokers watch from *The Bench:* Angus (Databroker), Reidmar (Teamster Rep.), Cmdr. Cutter (Tempest Co.), Yandee.

title: ST — Space Travel & Navigation
body:
• Ships use *Warp Cores* to jump through hyperspace between systems.
• *Jump Rating* determines how far per Warp Core consumed.
• Navigation checks use the *Systems* stat.
• Ships spend Fuel each month of travel.
• *Failed navigation:* 10% chance all navigation data wiped.
• Cryosleep is standard for long journeys.

Without Warp Cores, sub-light travel between systems takes *decades*.

title: Range Bands
body:
All weapons have a maximum effective range band. Attacks beyond that range are at the Warden's discretion — usually impossible or heavily penalised.

*Adjacent* — Arm's reach (melee). Unarmed, knives, hand tools.

*Close* — Same room or short corridor (~10m). Revolvers, shotguns, SMGs.

*Long* — Across a large open space (~50m). Pulse rifles, smart rifles, GPMGs.

*Extreme* — Very far (~100m+). Sniper rifles and heavy weapons.

Range is listed on each weapon's stat block. When in doubt: if you can throw something at it, it's Close. If you need a scope, it's Long or Extreme.
